entry.title: You need an MSI Installer for the SAP Business By Design Outlook Addin
entry.content:
You would like to have an MSI Installer rather then an exe Installer for the SAP Business ByDesign outlook Addin.

MSI Installer are not needed anymore as the Outlook Addin has the SAP Installer which includes the commands like /silent.

 

For more information please look here please, https://help.sap.com/sapsetup

 

 

alternatively if the Addin is already installed use NwSapSetup.exe /? to open the help instruction.

 

Our Plan is to move also the Excel Addin to the new installer.

Until that's done, for Excel we still provide an MSI Installer

entry.title: Re: QueryFailedError - Since Build Apps change 25th Feb
entry.content: I just created a new VCF entity and used in inside a new project. I was able to sort by my own UUID field. Could it be if I had an older database I can't now sort by such a field but a new database is OK?

entry.title: Search for the Root Cause of Inconsistency in /SCWM/AQUA
entry.content:
Introduction

During a Go-Live support phase, we encountered an inconsistency between physical and available stocks. While this issue is relatively rare—occurring perhaps once a day—it was still significant enough to investigate. Fixing it is usually straightforward with reports like /SCWM/CHM_LOG or /SCWM/CHM_PRF, but I wanted to understand the root cause. Unlike simple UI customization tasks, this case involved a deeper analysis, which made it particularly interesting for me.

Analyze

I decided to analyze the standard processes first. I waited until a new inconsistency appeared and started to check all related warehouse tasks for the Handling Unit and Product. As everyone knows, there should be no quant changes without a warehouse task. Finally, I was able to narrow down the time interval and analyze the application logs for this period. I found a strange error in the log, which I'll explain below.

The strange part was that the warehouse task was confirmed even though an error message was thrown in the log. Additionally, there were strange warehouse tasks confirmed with Product A when the Handling Unit had Product B. Luckily, this message was easy to find (Include /SCWM/LL03BFAF, form vquit_set_qty).

This form is called from the /SCWM/LL03BF0M form "QUITTIERUNG_PRUEFEN," which can be translated to "confirmation check." The vquit_set_qty is only called if cs_conf-vquit is not empty (I will return to this flag later). What is really interesting about this error message is the error handling. I show some other errors from these forms:

I asked Microsoft Copilot and ChatGPT to collect the statistics, unfortunately both failed with this task, so no nice statistic here, and you have to trust me or recheck 🙂 all messages (or almost all messages) lead to:

 

PERFORM undo_conf USING <conf> ct_tap.
DELETE ct_conf.

 

But not the

 

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE e224 WITH cs_conf-vlenr INTO gv_msgtext.
    CALL METHOD go_prot->add_message( ip_row = cs_conf-seqno ).
    EXIT.
  ENDIF.

 

So, long story short, this error message is not throwing confirmed warehouse tasks from the confirmation table.

Thoughts regarding the VQUIT Flag

And regarding the VQUIT (Confirmation in Bulk Storage Area: Removal of Complete HU) flag, where use shows that in standard it could be filled only from the IDOC ( /SCWM/IDOC_INPUT_WMTOCO )

Confirmation in Bulk Storage Area: Removal of Complete HU

Definition
Means that the entire handling unit (HU) is removed without remaining quantities or difference quantities.

One can imagine that SAP has no other option, since the HU probably was physically moved away and the WT must be confirmed accordingly, but in this case it would be nice to have corresponding AQUA update in my opinion.

Conclusion & Solution

The case was solved by identifying that the combination of using the VQUIT flag with a lack of validation in the app led to the inconsistency. One can learn that it is better not to use flags that are not used in standard processes.
Since it was unclear how exactly data validation was bypassed by the user, and considering the difficulty of changing all the places where VQUIT could be set, we decided to implement a simple fix. We changed all calls of the /SCWM/TO_CONFIRM to set the IV_COMMIT_WORK to abap_false and handled the commit ourselves, performing a rollback in case of error messages in the ET_BAPIRET. This adjustment helped, and no more inconsistencies were identified since then.

entry.title: Request: SAP CodeJam(s) in Hamburg, Germany on Friday Sept 26, 2025
entry.content:
Hello Dev Advocates team, 

@Mark_Teichmann @hgoerke and I are planning to host an SAP Inside Track in Hamburg, Germany on Saturday, Sept 27, 2025. We would like to combine this with one or ideally two CodeJams on Friday, Sept 26.

  • Location:
    • SAP Office - Hamburg, Germany
  • Topics in which we are mostly interested:
    • 1: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
    • 2: ABAP Cloud & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
    • Alternative for 1: Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and SAP generative AI Hub
    • Alternative for 2: Connecting Systems and Services Using SAP Integration Suite
  • Time frame:
    • Friday, Sept 26, 2025 (date fixed because of the SIT event the next day.)

Please reach out to me for questions and planning purposes. Looking forward to hearing from you.

Or can it be done per operation in a task list or work order?

entry.title: Re: Service Call Scheduling via SDK
entry.content:
Hello,

This is a child object of the ServiceCall. You work with it the same way that your are used to with the DocumentLines object. Here is a sample code:

The code is using the DI API, but I suggest you to use Service Layer. Please refer to the ServiceCalls Entity in Service Layer API Reference documentation.

Best regards

I then added S_SERVICE *. When I load up the Launchpad all looks good, the catalog are showing in the finder but when I click on the apps nothing shows, the screen stays blank. Any ideas what I'm missing here?

entry.title: Re: Datasphere - Populating Dynamic Default Values in Analytical Model Variables
entry.content:
Hello,

Your table function is incomplete and missing the necessary "RETURN SELECT ... FROM Table" statement, which is why you're encountering that error. Please complete the function and retest it. Please look at the below example to get an idea.

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition: Guidelines formula
entry.content:
Hi Ana,

Apologies for the two comments, my keyboard was acting up 🙂
So, you could have the guidelines set with a value which you want to be a percentage. 
For example, below i have 2 for Mentoring and Team Player.

Within the business rules on the spotAward object we create the following.

We create a variable to get the salary of the employee, have the rule set to always true or for a selected program only, and use the Then to set the award amount to be salary*guideline.
When I tested this on an employee who's salary is 413,000.000 I got 2% correctly.

Hope this helps,
Andres

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition: Guidelines formula
entry.content: Hi Anna,

entry.title: Re: API for purchase order to insert invoicing plan details
entry.content:
Hi,
we are trying to create an invoicing plan via API for a purchase order. We are using the following API:
SAP Business Accelerator Hub
Sending out the following JSON:
{
"InvoicingPlan": "51",
"InvoicingPlanType": "10",
"InvoicingPlanStartDate": "2024-12-01",
"InvoicingPlanEndDate": "2025-02-26",
"InvoicingPlanNextInvcDateRule": "51",
"PurchasingOrganization": "2310",
"PurchasingGroup": "COR",
"PurchaseOrderType": "NB",
"Plant": "2310"
}

Call ends with positive result, but nothing happen on S4H Public.

Any suggestion?

Thanks in advance

Alpho

entry.title: Re: Pricing Routine creation
entry.content: Will this BADI be used only in the Public Cloud, or also in the Private Cloud? In the Extensibility Registry, it states that the BADI is available for both Cloud and On-Premise. However, there seems to be no option to configure the BADI in either the On-Premise or Private Cloud environments

entry.title: Streamlining Dangerous Goods Compliance with SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Navigating the complex world of dangerous goods compliance just got easier with the latest update from SAP. On-Premise and Private Cloud customers can now receive new and updated dangerous goods compliance requirements and compliance requirement versions (CRV). This update ensures that the most current compliance requirement versions are at your fingertips, ready to be integrated into your system with minimal effort. Our Regulatory Content Service is available with SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 03 onward.

What’s New?

The new content delivery method allows for continuous updates of dangerous goods regulations embedded directly into SAP S/4HANA. This means that as soon as the system is connected to the content service, the latest compliance information is available for use, streamlining the process significantly compared to the previous method.

Activation Still Required

However, using the Activate Compliance Requirements – Dangerous Goods app, you still need to review and activate the new CRV. Otherwise, the new version will be in the system, but not in use. For more information on CRV activation, see the SAP Help Portal.

Future Developments

Stay tuned for updates found in our Roadmap, as we continue to enhance our services to better serve your needs.

We’re thrilled to offer this improved content delivery system, making dangerous goods compliance more efficient and manageable. For Regulatory Content Service setup information, refer to the relevant SAP Notes for public cloud, private cloud, and on-premise configurations. Setup information for the continuous delivery of dangerous goods regulations into SAP S/4HANA for product compliance can be found here.

Stay compliant, stay efficient, and let SAP S/4HANA guide you through the complexities of dangerous goods regulations with ease.

entry.title: SAP Joule use cases in Treasury
entry.content: I'd like to learn about any useful business use cases that leverage Joule to optimise Treasury operations.

entry.title: Re: BTP CPI. get Security Material in groovyscript problem
entry.content:
Hi, 
I tried the same, but as I want to have user and password secured, I did not want to switch to Secure Parameter. 
With this script I am able to read out also User Credentials.

I like to add Debug infos....not necessary, but nice to have.  

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import com.sap.it.api.ITApiFactory
import com.sap.it.api.securestore.SecureStoreService

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def map = message.getProperties()
    def debugCounter = map.keySet().findAll { it.startsWith("Debug_") }.size() + 1

    try {
        // Retrieve SecureStoreService instance (with null as the second argument)
        SecureStoreService secureStoreService = ITApiFactory.getService(SecureStoreService.class, null)
        if (secureStoreService == null) {
            message.setProperty("Debug_${debugCounter++}", "Error: SecureStoreService could not be instantiated.")
            return message
        }

        // Access stored user credentials
        def credential = secureStoreService.getUserCredential("Name_of_Credential")
        if (credential == null) {
            message.setProperty("Debug_${debugCounter++}", "Error: No credentials found for Credentials.")
            return message
        }

        // Extract username and password
        String username = credential.getUsername()
        String password = new String(credential.getPassword()) // Convert char array to String

        // Store debugging information
        message.setProperty("Debug_${debugCounter++}", "Successfully retrieved credentials from Secure Store.")
        message.setProperty("Username", username)
        message.setProperty("Password", password) 

    } catch (Exception e) {
        message.setProperty("Debug_${debugCounter++}", "Exception while accessing Secure Store: " + e.getMessage())
    }

    return message
}

entry.title: Re: see 0F document type data in reconciliation balance u...

entry.title: Re: Mongo ODBC Connection - failing to connect, Crystal 2...
entry.content: CR never retains the Password, huge security problem, anyone could open your report and change properties to access secure data. And just because Excel works doesn't automatically make every thing else work. You need to log the incident with their tech support....

entry.title: Re: Deficit of SL Unrestricted-use Message No. M7021
entry.content: For the inbound delivery that I posted, I think the issue comes from the MM module because even the MIGO cannot be processed. I have the impression that it is related to negative stock. Do you know what needs to be done for it to be accepted? thank you

entry.title: Bug in SAP Build Apps When Using Process FlowFunctions - Solution and Report
entry.content:
Bug Description: If you are developing in SAP Build Apps and use any of the FlowFunctions from the "Processes" tab (as shown in the attached image), you may encounter an issue when trying to deploy the application. Even if you do not actually use these functions and have only dragged one into the Logic Canvas, the "Mobile Services" tab becomes mandatory in the deployment configuration.

This behavior can confuse many users, as it suggests that configuring "Mobile Services" is necessary in the SAP BTP cockpit, which can lead to unnecessary work. Additionally, for users of the Free Edition, it may be impossible to resolve the issue directly in the cockpit.

Solution: Fortunately, there is a simple solution to remove this requirement without having to configure anything in SAP BTP:

  1. Go to App Settings → Authentication.
  2. Add the authentication method "Direct third party authentication", even if you are not going to use it.
  3. Save the application.
  4. Remove the "Direct third party authentication" method.
  5. Save the application again.

After these steps, the "Mobile Services" tab should no longer appear, and the deployment can proceed normally.

Purpose of the Post: With this post, I hope to help other developers who may encounter this issue and also inform the SAP Build Apps team about the bug.

SAP Build Apps

entry.title: Updating CSV Files in the SAP BI Repository
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to find a programmatic way of updating a csv file in the repository, specifically one that is used by a Webi report as a data source.  For Excel sources all is well.  There are RESTful calls to add a spread sheet to the repository (POST /spreadsheets) and to update it (PUT /spreadsheets/<spreadsheet_id>).  But for text\csv files there is a call to add a file to the repository (POST /infostore/folder/<folder_id>/file) but nothing to update it.  Using this call on a newer version of the csv file will create a new object in the repository with a new SI_ID.  I've thought of doing this and repointing existing Webis to the new repository object but there seems to be a gap in the RESTful SDKs too when it comes to repointing text dataproviders.

Can anybody give me hope that this is actually possible with the current SDKs, RESTful or otherwise? 

Many thanks,

Mike

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting to a subcontractor as a production ord...
entry.content:
Hi Prasath, thanks for your reply. I just want to understand the standard process from SAP.

for example ,

. Sales order is created.

. Production order is created against the sales order, where in production order we have 2 operations

Operation 1 in Warehouse, Operation 2 is done by the subcontractor, we will send the material to subcontractor, receive it back and then send it to end customer. 

 

for example,

1. Sales order > Prod.order > Operation X(Send to Subcon), here system will create a PR automatically? 

I just want to understand the standard MTO with an operation at the subcontractor, once the material is sent to subcontractor, he will do operations and material will be changed, will there be any goods issue at the subcontractor? what would be the document etc capturing the goods issue of raw material at subcontractor? And most important is that we will be sending E - Stock to subcontractor, do we need to have a specific storage location created for each subcontractor?

entry.title: Re: Effective dates are mismatched

Best regards,
Thorsten

entry.title: Unable to Rename Apps in SAP Build Apps - Free Edition
entry.content:
Issue Description: I can no longer find the option to rename an application in SAP Build Apps. I believe this functionality has either been moved or removed.

I am referring to renaming the app inside SAP Build Apps, not the name used during deployment.

Context: When I copy an app, the name automatically changes to "[previous_name] Copy". However, I am unable to modify the name afterward.

Request for Clarification: Has the option been moved to another section, or has it been removed entirely? If anyone knows how to rename an app, please share the steps.

@Dan_Wroblewski, could you confirm if this functionality has changed or do you know someone who might?

Sorry to bother you again, thank you in advance!

entry.title: Adaptive Transformation - Ein innovativer Ansatz für komplexe Veränderungsprojekte
entry.content:
In der jüngsten SAP Change und Training Kaffee Ecke folgten wir der Einladung von Eike Wagner auf seine Change Hut – seiner Skihütte im Zillertal, auf der Interessierte jederzeit zu einem Austausch über Change und Transformation willkommen sind.

Eike präsentierte das Konzept der adaptiven Transformation. Der Fokus lag auf modernen Ansätzen im Change Management, insbesondere im Kontext komplexer SAP-Implementierungen und Organisationsveränderungen.

Anfangs verwies er auf klassische Modelle wie Lewin (1947), Kübler-Ross (1969) und Kotter (1995) und erläuterte, warum diese in der heutigen dynamischen Geschäftswelt oft an ihre Grenzen stoßen. Auch neuere Konzepte wie Agilität, Co-Creation und New Work sind seiner Ansicht nach keine Allheilmittel, besonders wenn sie oberflächlich implementiert werden.

Zudem verändern technologische Möglichkeiten, besonders künstliche Intelligenz, die Funktionsweise von Organisationen grundlegend. Eike argumentierte, dass traditionelle Change-Modelle mit dieser Entwicklung oft nicht Schritt halten können.

 

Truly Adaptive Transformation

Als Denkanstoß stellte Eike das Konzept der "Truly Adaptive Transformation" vor. Dieses Modell geht über Agilität hinaus und berücksichtigt psychologische, soziologische und umweltbezogene Faktoren, die alle miteinander vernetzt sind. Diese 3 Themenbereiche beeinflussen unser Verhalten. Ziel ist es, viele Menschen schnell und umfassend zu Verhaltensänderungen zu bewegen. Dafür ist es nötig, die Determinanten hinter diesen Treibern zu identifizieren und deren Abhängigkeiten.

 

 

Eike betonte, dass jede organisationale Transformation im Kern bedeutet, dass viele Menschen so schnell und so umfassend wie möglich ihr Verhalten ändern müssen. Das Vorgehen der Truly adaptive Transformation ist laut Eike komplex, aber nicht kompliziert.

Eike ging in diesem Kontext auf die Unterscheidung zwischen komplexen und komplizierten Herausforderungen ein:  Er veranschaulichte dies am Beispiel eines Flugzeugtriebwerks (kompliziert, aber planbar und alle Faktoren sind bekannt) im Vergleich zu Mayonnaise (komplex, nicht rückgängig zu machen). Diese Differenzierung ist entscheidend für die Wahl des richtigen Vorgehensmodells in Transformationsprojekten.

Truly adaptive Transformation beginnt mit der Definition einer klaren Vision. Dabei gilt es  z.B. zu klären, was man mit der Einführung einer bestimmten Software wirklich erreichen möchte. Weiterhin ist die Identifikation notwendiger Technologien und Veränderungsbereiche nötig. Eike betonte die Wichtigkeit der Abhängigkeiten (kausal, zeitlich) der einzelnen Themen untereinander.

Das Vorgehen beinhaltet, dass autonome Teams für verschiedene Themenbereiche gebildet werden, die parallel mit unterschiedlichen Methoden arbeiten können. Eine regelmäßige übergreifende Synchronisation alle 2-3 Monate stellt die Kohärenz sicher. Dabei ist es nicht relevant, dass die Teams alle agil arbeiten. Die Vorgehensweise (agil oder nach dem linearen Wasserfallmodell) der einzelnen Teams hängt vom Thema und der Sinnhaftigkeit ab. Die Frage ist: Welche Arbeitsweise hilft dem Team, um das Ergebnis, den Auftrag, den sie haben, gut zu erreichen. Manchmal ist es agil und manchmal ist es eben nicht agil, unabhängig davon, was sich die Teams wünschen.

Auf der obigen Folie ist das Vorgehen nochmals illustriert.

Eikes Empfehlung war, alle relevanten Faktoren einer Transformation zu sammeln: Neben der Technologie, Tools / Systeme (bei einer Technologie-Implementierung gesetzt), hilfreiche / notwendige Strukturveränderung,  Prozesse, die im System abgebildet werden sollen, und Prozesse, die an das System anzupassen sind sowie Mindset Skills Wissen, Knowledge der Mitarbeitenden.

Dabei betonte Eike die Wichtigkeit der echten Einbindung der Teilnehmenden.

Er zeigte eine toolbasierte Visualisierung von OKRs (Objectives & Key Results) auf Jahresebene und auf Quartalssebene, wobei Abhängigkeiten zwischen den Jahreszielen und den Quartalszielen dargestellt wurden. Basierend darauf kann in Abhängigkeit von den Schwerpunkten, die sich Teams setzen direkt visuell festgestellt werden, ob zu viele Abhängigkeiten in den parallelen Teams vorhanden sind und damit die Wahrscheinlichkeit erhöht ist, zu scheitern. Damit wird deutlich, dass entweder die Ziele angepasst werden müssen oder, wenn das nicht möglich / gewünscht ist, die Komplexität bewusst in Kauf zu nehmen und dafür die Abstimmungen in kürzeren Abständen zu halten.

 

Eike stellte danach das Konzept des GuP (Gestaltungs- und Umsetzungspaket, im Original Design and Implementation Package, DIP) vor. Dieses erlaubt die parallele Gestaltung und Umsetzung verschiedener Aspekte der Transformation. Er betonte die Wichtigkeit, frühzeitig über organisatorische Anpassungen nachzudenken, selbst wenn die finale Softwarekonfiguration bei einer IT-Transformation noch nicht feststeht.

Eine wichtige Rolle in Eikes Modell spielt der Chief Transformation Designer. Diese Position ist verantwortlich für die Vernetzung der Teams und schützt vor übermäßiger Beeinflussung durch das Top-Managements. Eike unterstrich die Bedeutung dieser Rolle für den Erfolg von Transformationsprojekten.

Diskussion

In der Diskussion wurden verschiedene Aspekte in der Praxis beleuchtet. Eine Teilnehmerin fragte nach Eikes Einschätzung zu den Modellen von Holt (Change Readiness) und Venkatesh (UTAUT-Modell), die den Change begünstigen sollen, und sprach die Notwendigkeit der Veränderung, soziale Unterstützung, Selbstwirksamkeit usw. an. Eikes Einschätzung differenzierte zwischen individueller und organisationaler Veränderungsbereitschaft. Sollte in der Organisation die Veränderungsbereitschaft fehlen, könne man versuchen, diese zu erhöhen oder die Veränderung zu starten und mit Change- und Trainingsinterventionen zu begleiten. Eike empfiehlt dabei, ins Tun zu kommen - etwas machen und verproben, inwieweit die Bereitschaft wirklich gering ist wie angenommen oder nicht, und daran anknüpfend den nächsten Schritt zu planen.

Eike teilte auch Erfahrungen aus realen Projekten, wie eine SAP-Implementierung bei BMW vor über 20 Jahren. Er beschrieb, wie ein Rollenspiel des Rekrutierungsprozesses mit dem Angeln hinter einer Stellwand zu einem Aha-Moment bei den Beteiligten führte und die Notwendigkeit für Veränderung verdeutlichte.

Ein weiterer Diskussionspunkt war die Integration mit bestehenden Methoden wie SAFe oder LeSS. Eike erklärte, dass sein Modell Elemente dieser Frameworks integrieren könne, aber flexibler in der Anpassung an spezifische Projektanforderungen sei.

Bei SAP Beratern gilt es beispielsweise auch, sich an die Methodik des Kunden anzupassen. Nutzt dieser SAFe, sollten man auch danach arbeiten, auch wenn man aus verschiedenen Gründen das jeweilige Modell nicht präferiert.

Zudem gab es eine längere Diskussion zu den Ansätzen der Agilität vs. der linearen Methodik, auch Wasserfall genannt. So meinte zum Beispiel Torben Mauch, der übrigens auch auf der Change Hut anwesend war, dass manchmal Wasserfall Modelle durchaus Sinn machen und man dies nicht ideologisch sehen sollte.

Abschließend betonte Eike, dass die zunehmende Vielfalt an Möglichkeiten und die Geschwindigkeit von Veränderungen flexiblere Ansätze im Change Management erfordern. Er ermutigte die Teilnehmer, bewusst über ihr Vorgehen bei Transformationsprojekten nachzudenken und die Methode basierend auf der spezifischen Situation und Komplexität des Projekts zu wählen.

Die Session endete mit einem Ausblick auf die kommenden Kaffeecken:

Global vernetzt: Change Kommunikation für eine weltweite SAP S/4HANA Transformation am 20.03.: https://events.sap.com/de/sap-change-training/de/home

und

SAP PoC - Der Einsatz von KI bei der Erstellung von Trainingsbedarfsanalysen und Lerninhalten am 24.04. https://go4.events.sap.com/eur-kaffee-ecke-april/de_de/home.html

SAVE the date - Das jährliche Community Treffen am 25.6. wird demnächst zur Registrierung geöffnet.

Wer sich über Eikes Arbeit informieren will, findet hier weitere Infos

Eikes Website https://changeworkout.rocks

Eikes Change Community https://changeworkout.community

entry.title: SAP CAL S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully Activated Appliance
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The SAP CAL appliance is with the status Active and I cannot connect to it (RDP or SAP GUI). 

As soon as I click on Connect and enter my login credentials, the following error message appears:

partner '108.....:sapdp00' not reached

WSAEWOULDBLOCK: Resource temporarily unavailable 

Could you please help me? Thank you.

entry.title: SAC settlement time variance best practices
entry.content:
Hi, 
Is there any best practice for a reconciliation model including a calculation of a settlement time variance. something like that:

Settlement Variance=Actual Settlement Time−Expected Settlement Time

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Error "Serial number already exists for material" whe...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your suggestion

I`ve tried it, but there is not really a possibility to link the existing serial number with the equipment with this menu.

entry.title: S4HANA Conversion - FINS_MIG_STATUS -MUJ Step
entry.content:
Hello,
I am running FINS_MIG_STATUS t-code. In MUJ step, I have got some errors. After I will fix these error message, how can I also migrate data which are getting error before? Most data has been migrated to table/s. If I restart MUJ step, will there duplicated data in ACDOCA table or anyone else?

Regards, thanks.

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @AdrianDBorja . Regarding issue 1: are using HANA Cloud db in the Free Tier or BTP Trial, which have some restrictions: https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-administration-guide/sap-hana-database-license#free-tier-restrictions?

Regards.

entry.title: Issue with Billing Costs for External Workers in "Manage Project Billing"
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We are currently encountering an issue in the "Manage Project Billing" app where billing costs are not being calculated for external workers. This occurs even when the external workers are:

  1. Assigned to the project and planned within a work package, and

  2. Purchased separately via a purchase order with an service entry sheet.

Steps Taken So Far:

  1. We created a project with a work package and assigned an external worker to it.

step 2 - We created a purchase order and included the external worker in it.

step 3 - We record 2 hours of work for the external worker. These 2 hours are also visible as a time entry in the project. However, this time recording is not mapped to the external worker role "$ Time / Fee Type 10 (T010)." We don't understand why.

Note: The name begins with "$" because the system currently does not have an English translation for the original German term.

step 4 - after we created a time record for the external worker a Service Entry Sheet and a goods receipt are automatically created:

step 5 - Now if we try to create a Billing Document for the Project no Billing Costs are estimated even though the Price List for the Role "$ Time / Fee type 10" is maintained

Do you have any idea what we do wrong in our process?

Thank you 

Best Regards

Mark

entry.title: Re: Fixed Asset Version 9.0 PL09
entry.content: Ensure that the depreciation area ID does not contain any special characters. Use only alphanumeric characters. This was my case the code was "razão" and it supose to be "razao" or any other without special characteres. Unfortunately, it was already imported and runing some activities, so I had to register a ticket with SAP in order to fix it.

entry.title: Re: Identity authentication to different Corporate Idp based on email domains in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Is there equivalent functionality for self-hosted? #SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform 4.3 Support Pack 2 Patch 10 Version : 14.3.2.4537.10

entry.title: Year-End Process Duplicated – Retained Earnings Posted Twice
entry.content:
We've encountered an issue where the year-end process was duplicated. Two different users had the year-end closing window open simultaneously.

Initially, they were troubleshooting some errors, and both ended up executing the process. Instead of SAP preventing the duplicate execution or crashing for one of them, the system allowed both executions, resulting in the same amount being posted to Retained Earnings twice.

Has anyone experienced this issue before? Any insights on why this happened?

Thanks

entry.title: Termination screen attachment mandatory issue
entry.content:
Hi Experts ,

We have a scenario where employee terminates voluntarily by providing termination reason in termination screen at this time attachment section is mandatory but when manager sent back the request the attachment section becomes non mandatory what are the possible ways to fix the issue.Kindly help.

 

Regards

Anu

entry.title: SAP EBS: Question on Interpretation Algorithm "20 020: Document Number Search"
entry.content:
In one SAP client, we use are successfully using posting rules with interpretation algorithm "20 020: Document Number Search" to automatically clear open payment documents. The bank accounting documents and payment documents are posted to the same clearing account so the above algorithm does not have any issues finding open payment documents (as long as the amount and document numbers match) and clearing them automatically.

Now we are implementing EBS in another client and we have set up posting rules in a similar way using algorithm "20 020: Document Number Search" for clearing of open payment documents. However, here payment documents are posted on one clearing account (ABC) and the bank accounting documents are posted to another clearing account (XYZ). Essentially two different clearing accounts are used instead of one for clearing of outgoing payments. And during testing I cannot get the above algorithm to automatically clear open payment documents during electronic bank statement processing.

My question is: can it be that SAP interpretation algorithms only work as long as both a bank accounting document (that is being posted) and an open payment document (that I want the algorithm to find and clear) are on the same clearing account (ABC or XYZ)? And if two or more clearing accounts are used the algorithm will not find those open documents to clear?

entry.title: Create an SAP plugin for both SAP BTP and on-premise Launchpad
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community

Recently migrated from SAP WebIDE to BAS?

BAS is definitely missing the plugin template with SAP WebIDE had. Worry not. You can still create your own plugin that will work perfectly on both on-prem and cloud launchpads

Step 1:

Create a Freestyle SAPUI5 in SAP BAS

The plugin is usually a button that redirects to an external website or opens a popup. So we want it to load as soon as the launchpad loads.Here we are creating a plugin to redirect to an external website. We are writing all logic in component.js

Step 2:

open manifest.json.Change type to "component" from "application".

Add sap.flp section as shown below

Step 3:

Create renderer in component.js using this function

 

 

_getRenderer: function () {
                var that = this,
                    oDeferred = new jQuery.Deferred(),
                    oRenderer;
                that._oShellContainer = jQuery.sap.getObject( "sap.ushell.Container" );
                if ( !that._oShellContainer ) {
                    oDeferred.reject(
                        "Illegal state: shell container not available; this component must be executed in a unified shell runtime context." );
                } else {
                    oRenderer = that._oShellContainer.getRenderer( "fiori2" );
                    if ( oRenderer ) {
                        oDeferred.resolve( oRenderer );
                    } else {
                        // renderer not initialized yet, listen to rendererCreated event
                        that._onRendererCreated = function ( oEvent ) {
                            oRenderer = oEvent.getParameter( "renderer" );
                            if ( oRenderer ) {
                                oDeferred.resolve( oRenderer );
                            } else {
                                oDeferred.reject( "Illegal state: shell renderer not available after recieving 'rendererLoaded' event." );
                            }
                        };
                        that._oShellContainer.attachRendererCreatedEvent( that._onRendererCreated );
                    }
                }
                return oDeferred.promise();
            }

 

 

 

Step 4: Call the renderer in init function of component.js and write your  logic there

 

 

        init: function () {
                var rendererPromise = this._getRenderer();
var url="www.google.com";
                rendererPromise.then( function ( oRenderer ) {
                    oRenderer.addHeaderEndItem( "sap.ushell.ui.shell.ShellHeadItem", {
                        icon: "sap-icon://cloud",
                        tooltip: "Google",
                        press: function ( event ) {
                            sap.m.URLHelper.redirect( url, true );
                        }
                    }, true, true );
                } );
            },

 

 

Your plugin application is now ready to be configured in both your on-prem and cloud launchpad.

Step 5: Open SAP GUI /ui2/flpd_cust 

Create new Catalog and add 2 different target mappings as shown 

 

In parameters give this parameter "renderers/fiori2/componentData/config/enableHelp" and default value as true

Step 6: Configure in BTP

 

 

Add sap.cloud settings in manifest.json

 "sap.cloud": {
    "public": true,
    "service": "yourapplicationidincomponent"
  }

 

 

In BTP open content manager and add this plugin to your launchpad content

Assign it to the roles you want to add it to.Logout and login again

 

Result: You will see a cloud icon as soon as you load the launchpad

entry.title: Import Financial Plan Data - Understanding the plan data scope
entry.content:
The Import Financial Plan Data app is available in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private edition. It allows key users to import plan data line items from CSV files.

Understanding Plan Data Scope

This blog post will explain how the plan data scope works and how it helps you avoid accidently overwriting data.
Please keep in mind that the Import Financial Plan Data app is only an interface for uploading data into the system. The data has to be maintained and reviewed manually before uploading it, as  only technical checks are performed (for example, checking whether master data exists in the target system) and no business logic is integrated.

Let us explore how to use the Import Financial Plan Data app in a simple scenario for cost center planning.

Example Scenarios

Example 1: Initial Import
In the example provided, you intend to import four plan data records. Since this is the first time the application is being used, no records are present in the database. This means four new records will be incorporated from the CSV file, as shown in the following screens:

After importing the plan data, you then see a success message that allows you to run the Display Financial Plan Data report directly. This report displays the plan data on the corresponding accounts, indicating that the outcome is as expected.

Example 2: Correcting Data
Suppose you notice that one of the amounts in your file was incorrect. To correct this, simply change the amount from 100 to 150 USD, and import the file again. The import process informs you that the existing plan data will be adapted within the plan data scope and that four records will be incorporated from the CSV file.

After the successful import, the Display Financial Plan Data report shows the corrected amount of 150 USD.

Example 3: Adding Missing Accounts
In another example, you may have forgotten to upload the plan data for a specific account. In this case, you merely need to reimport the file, including the new account.

The system notifies you that the existing plan data will be adapted within the plan data scope and that five records will be incorporated from the CSV file.
Consequently, the Display Financial Plan Data report now displays the plan data for the missing account as well.

 

Example 4: Handling Complex Scenarios
Let us now examine how the system handles more complex scenarios or repetitive imports.
To reverse specific records before importing a new CSV file, the system creates a logical query based on the dimensions included in the file. Dimensions marked with an X in row #3 are concatenated with AND, while the different members in this column are concatenated with OR. For dimensions without an X, the members are not specified, so records for all members of that dimension are reversed.

Note:
This may reverse data values for which there is no corresponding row in the source file because the selection defined by the plan data scope reverses all values for which the following is true, for example:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025"
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"
AND (Cost Center = "0017101101" OR Cost Center = "17101201")

In example 3, you mistakenly planned on cost center 1710201, which should not have been planned on. The data you imported in this file is incorrect, and now you wish to send only three records instead of five.
To correct this, you reimport the file with these three records, but you leave the X for the cost center dimension in the file.

This action results in the reversal of the records meeting the following criteria:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025"
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"
AND Cost Center = "0017101101"

The erroneous cost center data remains unchanged, so the incorrect data for cost center 1710201 is still present in the system after this import.

To correct the erroneous data, you need to remove the X from the cost center column.

This action results in the reversal of the records meeting the following criteria:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025"
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"

This sets the records for cost centers not specified in the CSV file to zero.

 

Example 5: Updating and Adding Records
In this last example, you want to correct one existing record for 2025 from 150 USD to 200 USD and add new records for 2026.

Let’s assume that the data in your system looks as follows:

By importing the CSV file as shown above, the system reverses the plan records for both cost centers and both years. Note, however, that even records that were not part of the uploaded CSV file are also reversed because of the following criteria:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND (General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025" OR General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2026")
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"
AND (Cost Center = "0017101101" OR Cost Center ="0017101201")

To resolve this, you need to split your data into two upload files.

One CSV file contains the record that needs to be corrected and where the X is set for all dimensions, including the G/L account.

This changes the amount from 150 USD to 200 USD, while all other records remain unchanged.

The second file contains the new records for 2026, where you don’t need to set the X for the G/L account dimension.

After both CSV files have been uploaded, the data in your system looks as follows:

Conclusion:
To prevent data from being overwritten unintentionally, uploaded data must be disjointed. A clear understanding of the plan data scope ensures that records can be added, updated, and reversed safely.

entry.title: Re: Error Importing Fixed Assets from Excel
entry.content:
Ensure that the depreciation area ID does not contain any special characters. Use only alphanumeric characters. This was my case the code was "razão" and it supose to be "razao" or any other without special characteres. So, even I've had a unique depreciation area registered it was not recognized from the import file, because it assume you didn't select all the active areas of the asset class.  Unfortunately for me, it was already imported and run some process, so I had to register a ticket with SAP in order to fix it.
TIPS FOR SAP: SAP should not allow the user define any table id/code, and let them personalize their names only.

entry.title: Re: Value Help with parameter does not open in Parameter...
entry.content: Thank you! Yes, we already using this - it's for sure one of the better places to check. We also already are comparing the showcase app installed in our system to what we are trying to achieve and we can't find a real difference. Our assumptions are that the basic data types ( abap.char(x) ) need to be the same. Foreign key definitions for the additional bound fields seem not to be mandatory when checking examples here and there. But as said, that's just assumptions as this is not explained anywhere.

entry.title: How to Derive the Duration in Years between 2 dates in SAP Subscription Billing?
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to understand the Duration Calculation component and need assistance with deriving the number of years between two dates.

 

I have configured the system as follows:

  • I am using the Computed Property "Years" as an input to the table. The table data is maintained according to the screenshot below.

     

  • The fields are mapped in "Calculate Price Element" as indicated in the configuration details below.

 

When creating a subscription, I have set the subscription start date to 01.01.2024, but I am receiving a value of 0 in the pricing calculation.

 

Could you please help me understand why this is happening and how I can resolve it?

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Ankit

entry.title: Re: Unable to Rename Apps in SAP Build Apps - Free Edition
entry.content: Perhaps I am missing something ... do you mean this renaming?

entry.title: Re: Deficit of SL Unrestricted-use Message No. M7021
entry.content: Is that a regular inbound delivery or maybe stock transport order or return delivery or ...?

entry.title: Re: Import Fixed asset Master Data From Microsoft Excel
entry.content:
You must have all ACTIVE depreciation areas (COLGAP and NIIF) in your txt file. it's mean you will have two lines to the same item. Also there are  some prerequisites for the different types of assets you want to import. Please, have a look on this documentation: https://help.sap.com/saphelp_sbo900/helpdata/EN/94/7e131c04ca428d933f5375a98aef0e/content.htm?no_cache=true

Also, ensure that the depreciation area ID does not contain any special characters. Use only alphanumeric characters. This was my case the code was "razão" and it supose to be "razao" or any other without special characteres. So, even I've had a unique depreciation area registered it was not recognized from the import file, because it assume you didn't select all the active areas of the asset class.  Unfortunately for me, it was already imported and run some process, so I had to register a ticket with SAP in order to fix it.
TIPS FOR SAP: SAP should not allow the user define any table id/code, and let them personalize their names only.

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition: Guidelines formula
entry.content:
So, you could have the guidelines set with a value which you want to be a percentage. 
For example, below i have 2 for Mentoring and Team Player.

 

Within the business rules on the spotAward object we create the following.

 

We create a variable to get the salary of the employee, have the rule set to always true or for a selected program only, and use the Then to set the award amount to be salary*guideline.
When I tested this on an employee who's salary is 413,000.000 I got 2% correctly.

Hope this helps,
Andres

entry.title: Re: Updating CSV Files in the SAP BI Repository
entry.content: How about saving the files in a shared network-folder and using this as source?

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @AdrianDBorja , Thanks for the feedback.

For 1) massive=True allows to train multiple time series forecast with a single command. In the blog it is used in combination with the group_key to forecast indidvidual values for each country. Maybe you get the error when setting massive=True but without providing that group_key?

For 2) The group_key accepts indeed only a string, not a list or an array. This means you just need to concat the values of the multiple grouping columns together into one column. The name of this new column is then the group_key. Here is an example:

df_remote.select('*', ("REGION || '-' || COUNTRYOFRESIDENCE", 'CONCATCOL'))

For 3) In case you already have a SELECT statement (which might join multiple tables) you can base the hana_ml DataFrame on that custom sql.

df_remote = conn.sql('Your SELECT statement...')

Alternatively you can also use the hana_ml package to join multiple hana_ml DataFrames. Something like:

df_remote = conn.table('USEDCARS').set_index('CAR_ID') \
.join(df_rem_accepted.select(['CAR_ID', 'PREDICTED']) \
.set_index('CAR_ID'), how='left')

For 4) df_rem_countries is created in the Notebook that has all the code. Thanks for letting me know, I will make that clearer in the text

https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-global-center-of-excellence-samples/blob/main/Hands-on%20Tutorial%20Machine%20Learning%20with%20SAP%20HANA%20Cloud/030%20Multiple%20time-series%20forecast.ipynb

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content: @Vitaliy-R this is enterprise acct actually

entry.title: Re: When I move to Quality system from Dev my BW Query OD...
entry.content: did you capture those in tr?

entry.title: Re: Need to Print Smartform on Zebra Bluetooth Printer ZQ320 from Mobile Computer TC58E
entry.content: If it's a custom program, nobody can help here, ask your local developer.

entry.title: Re: Anyone implemented Inspector Qualification?
entry.content: The inspector qualification is rather for documentation purposes. Your use case is not yet entirely clear...where should the check takes place? Brw. there is a similar requirement on the topic on the Customer Influence Portal https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/328791/

entry.title: Carrier Determination - SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 TM
entry.content:
As announced in SAP S/4HANA PcE 2023 FPS03 (https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-fp03-of-transportation-management-in-s-4hana-cloud-private/ba-p/13950181), it is now possible to determine the carrier in the Freight Order, Freight Unit, and Consignment Order at the time of document creation.

This functionality is particularly beneficial in cases where not all advanced TM planning features are utilized. Additionally, it allows users to allocate their time to more critical aspects of the process.

Below I describe a little about it:

  • General Activations and/or Checks
  • Configuration Steps for Functionality Execution
  • Extension
  • New fields in cockpit
  • Carrier propagation

General Activations and/or Checks

1. Freight Unit Stage List, FU/PU Stage List - Field Catalog

2. Freight Unit Stage Hierarchy, FU/PU Stage Hierarchy, Consignment Order List, Consignment Order Hierarchy - Field Catalog

3. Freight Order List, Freight Order Hierarchy, Freight Order / Freight Booking List - Field Catalog

4. Activation of the "Document Mass Change" button - Page layout

5. New Grouping Rule

 

Configuration Steps for Functionality Execution

1. Create Carrier Determination Profile

2. Assign Carrier Determination

Profile in Freight Unit

Freight Order

Consignment Order

If using the Transportation Cockpit and intending to group all demand by "Carrier from Carrier Determination

Create Requirement Grouping Profile

 

Extension

BADI /SCMTMS/EX_TSPD

This BAdI offers three key intervention possibilities:

  • Adjust the carrier determination profile applied to a given business document.
  • Modify the decision on whether redetermination is necessary.
  • Adjust the determined carrier.

 

New Fields in Transportation Cockpit

 

Carrier Propagation

1. Classic Planning: When the Freight Unit (FU) is assigned to a Freight Order (FO), the carrier information is propagated between FU and FO.

2. Multiple Carriers: If two FUs with different carriers exist, the FO will inherit the carrier from the first FU.


3. Post-Planning Adjustments: If an FO is already planned and FUs are removed, the system will retain the carrier from the first FU after modifications.

4. Manual Carrier Assignment: If a carrier is manually populated in the FO, the system will not override it with the FU's carrier information.

entry.title: CSD View - Purchase Order Items Goods Receipt
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I would like to create a CDS view with the “Custom CDS View” app. My goal is to list all purchase order items that are overdue.
I have already found the view I_PurOrdScheduleLineAPI01 to determine the delivery date. However, I am still missing the information as to whether a goods receipt has taken place. Where can I get this from?

Thanks in advance.

 

MM Purchasing Sourcing and Procurement - Purchase Contract Management

entry.title: Re: Bar chart output is in a graded colour
entry.content:
try another color-palette
or
change the formatting of the bar in the chart from automatic to individual and change the color.

entry.title: Customer Hierarchy In Condition Contract
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Wanted to know whether we need to have Pricing and Rebates Ticks (Green Check marks) in VDH1N for Hierarchy Nodes, even in S4 Hana, so that rebates pricing can be calculated for Hierarchy node at item level in the Condition contract.

Appreciate your inputs. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Fiori: where to set email for user
entry.content:
Hi Expert:

Where to set email for each user in Fiori?

entry.title: Re: How to share data between a plug-in and the application?
entry.content: Hello Marian,

entry.title: Does anyone have a workaround for null suppression in a waterfall graph?
entry.content:
In SAP Analytics Cloud I want to remove measures from a waterfall that are null. Given filtering on e.g. profit centers, a measure might end up being null and if the filter changes, the measure might take on a positive or negative value and should then be included again.

Datasource is a SAP Datasphere Analytical Model.

Does anyone have a workaround for null suppression in a waterfall graph? As per KBA 3150383 this is not available even though it is possible to enable zero suppression (which does not seem to work either, see screen shots). A couple of screen shots has been attached for context. 

SAC table of datasource without zero or null suppression.

Same table WITH zero and null suppression. All ok.

Waterfall WITH zero suppression. I want to get rid of the highlighted elements. For some reason, Consulting Fee Recovery is not removed with zero suppression.

Thanks for your help.

entry.title: Re: How to share data between a plug-in and the application?
entry.content: Hello Marian, thank you for reading my question. Of course, a fast backend would solve it. But even if it would be fast i would load data twice instead of sharing it between plugin and app. I guess i have to stick with EventHub...

entry.title: Re: Controlling Data Replication by CR Type
entry.content: Use Tcode DRFF and set the filter criteria on Change Request Type

entry.title: SAP HANA Database Server HDB Service Stopping Unexpectedly
entry.content:
Dear SAP Tech users,

For the past few weeks, our SAP HANA DB Server's HDB service has been stopping unexpectedly every other day. To resolve this, I manually log in and run the following command:

/usr/sap/NDB/HDB00/exe/sapcontrol -nr 00 -function RestartSystem HDB
Has anyone else encountered this issue? What could be causing the HDB service to stop, and are there any permanent solutions to prevent this from happening?

entry.title: Re: BW transfer data to 3rd party system Oracle/MSSQL with field value filled with space
entry.content: did you check the transformation-rules in BW from S/4 to BW and also BW to OH ?

entry.title: Re: Using IPS to create a predictable password for non-SS...
entry.content:
Hi

The simplest way to achieve this is what is called "password migration"

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-source-system-to-migrate-user-passwords-from-sap-successfactors-systems-to-identity-authentication

With this scenario your non-SSO will be able to login to SSFF with their old password (SSFF Password) and the passwords will be saved and managed in IAS after the first logon.

Create new passwords and share with the users anyway is not a good idea. As a second option i'd use an activation email if the password synch is not achievable in your scenario.

I'd recommend also to join the call "SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS): Open Office Hours for SAP SuccessFactors HCM". There's one session tomorrow. You'll be able to raise your questions there. Check the links at the top of the blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/migration-to-sap-cloud-identity-authentication-ias-ips-innovation-alert-amp/ba-p/271407

 

Hope it helps

Diego

entry.title: Re: VC Material variants - Multiple Values
entry.content:
Hello,


Did you find any solution to this problem ?

Thank you in advance,

entry.title: SAC Date Calculation in DA
entry.content:
I have a “payment method” dimension with an attribute for “Expected Settlement Time”. Based on the method chosen the attribute can be 1/2/3/4 days (the attribute is set as an integer).

I have a measure for incomes, a measure for outcomes and one created to copy the income in the expected settlement date, named “Expected Outgoing”.

My problem is that I don’t know how to write this calculation - copying the some 3-4 days after based on the attribute.

I’ve tried the below, but the syntax isn’t correct, as it mixes date and number of days:

DATA([d/Measures] = "Expected_Outgoing") = 
RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Income", [d/Date] = DATEDIFF( [d/Date],[d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD].[p/STLMNT_TIME],"DAY" ) )

DATA([d/Measures] = "Expected_Outgoing") =

RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Income", [d/Date] = DATEDIFF( [d/Date], [d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD].[p/STLMNT_TIME], "DAY" ) )

 

How should I approach that?

entry.title: Re: Fiori: where to set email for user
entry.content:
Hi,

You can administer the email-adress in transaction SU01.

Nils

entry.title: Re: List Report Controller Extension - custom button plus...
entry.content:
Hi @Marian_Zeis 

Do you know why your Fiori tools show the button "Add Controller Extension" but mine doesn't?

Is it a cloud-only option, do we have to be in oData v4 or is there another minimum requirement?

Thanks

entry.title: What are the RISE datacenter locations in EUROPE providing EU Access
Re: Critical Success Factors for a Seamless SAP S/4HANA Transition
entry.content: very interesting

entry.title: Re: Fiori: where to set email for user
entry.content: @kalleanka123 I have set in SU01 but still not show. Pls advise next step,

entry.title: Error while uploading Job Req data via integration center
entry.content:
Hi,

When trying to import data in Job Requisition via integration center, getting errors in integration center job

Error 1.JobRequisition: Mandatory value is missing for deleted for record jobReqId-we have got the values whether deleted or not deleted.

Error 2- when maintained data and trying to run the job getting another error -JobRequisition: UPSERT failed for the row with key: . Reason: Field deleted not valid for templateId id, . The row will not be processed for subsequent entities.

Upon checking we can see there is no field in template.

entry.title: Collective order in SAP - target and actual costs
entry.content:
Hi All,

 

I have a scenario, where I have order with couple of suborders. Confirmation is done by external MES system. Each of suborders are confirmed separately via MES. Control key in routings set with automatic GR. Coll. order with goods movement is activated in cost controlling in my production order type. While confirming  the suborder 101 movement is done and 261. 

The problem is between the end of the month and the beginning of the other one. Imagine the situation when all of suborders are confirmed in one month and the main order is confirmed in the next month. Then while having cost report in kkbc_ord (limited to the previous month) I cannot see the target's cost in suborders. There are 'artificial' variances, which will disappear after confirming the order in next month.

Is there any chance to have these target costs in suborders? 

Regards, thank you for your help!

entry.title: The ABAP Detective and the Errant Electrolyte
entry.content:
An aspect of sustainability is minimal resource use, in both the sense of lower costs, but also the avoidance of energy use that contributes to carbon dioxide emissions from fossil fuels. For an establishment on "all-electric" the latter is what might be called an externality. As a code and systems analysis detective, I look for areas of improvement.

Previous investigations into environmental monitoring tools are noted at the end; this case focuses on the efficiency and use of domestic hot water delivery. In the backwaters of the united states, standard appliances include a 30 or 40 gallon (sorry: X liters) tank with a heating source constantly attempting to maintain a set minimum temperature. Other areas use the "on-demand" approach, or hot-water-as-a-service, with a heating device close to the delivery point (let's not go into the 110 versus 220 volt infrastructure as a distraction).

To the crux of the biscuit: I set up a temperature monitor directly above the output of the hot water tank, connected to a Raspberry Pi that is polled by a central system. Other approaches would be locally collecting the data and uploading in scheduled bursts, pushing rather than pulling.

TMP102 gauge, copper, insulation. (Photo by Jim Spath)

(photo by Jim Spath)

This ad hoc wiring approach works in a non-intrusive way, as the sensor unit does not touch the copper pipes, nor contact running water. Typically throughout the day, hot water is not running, so the readings are close to ambient room temperature. When a tap is opened somewhere, preheated water goes near enough to tick up the temperature reading, then after the flows stops the temperature gradually degrades. Or, if the tank completely empties of heated water, the readings will correspondingly decline.

Standalone sensor:

TMP102 gauge with blips

(screenshot from Zabbix web client)

A few times during the day, temperature rises, then declines. Not a particularly useful data set if you want to ask questions like "how hot is the hot water" or "how long did the washer run?" or even "how much did this unit of work cost?". The human brain can glance at these shapes and discern patterns, like Sherlock Holmes checking out cigar-ash-flakes in a pursuit. Writing an algorithm is a bit trickier.

The Zabbix open source suite I'm using has tools to run calculations on data feeds; for instance in some cases I'd pull values in Celsius, then add a derived parameter converting to Fahrenheit. There are ways to "look back" to prior values to determine deltas (which I've not implemented yet). You can also set up triggers to perform actions such as alerting on worst case scenarios (like overheating or frozen pipes: Ow!).

I'll gloss over the gnarly details of the Sparkfun/QWIIC monitoring hardware/software and cut to the chase: only one specific gauge per Pi, without adding multiplexing I2C complexities, so I was faced with deploying 2 Pi's with the same gauge type, or add a different gauge on 1 Pi. I chose the latter. Then the case went into what we colloquially called a "mung" state. Not "hung" but not right either.

Pi and the OS

If I could, I'd "flame" here, showing the rapid pace of software development breaks things that worked a few short years ago in surprising ways. I'm using code that someone else wrote in bash shell scripting back in 2012 or so, and that logic doesn't need to be tweaked when a new Linux kernel or even a subsystem component is written. And generally, Python examples the vendors share keeps working from year to year. Upgrades to the running environment are non-breaking other than minor steps like "oh, use Python 3.12 now instead of 3.10" that are easy enough to resolve.

My first Pi platform had a "6.1" 32-bit Linux kernel version; more recently new installs have a "6.6" kernel, where 64-bit flavors are as stable as the 32-bit. Great, why would that matter? Somewhere in the matrix, Python grew an appendage, nicknamed "venv" meaning a virtual environment. Let's add containers, crates, and virtualization while we're at it. Except this is a $10 machine with a single purpose.

TMP102 and BME280

These 2 temperature sensors are available in the chosen "ecosystem" that connects the Pi brain to the digital sensors, which could also include humidity and barometric pressure. Since I had collected and deployed at least one of each, I assumed connecting both to 1 Pi would be simple. I had code samples in shell and Python for both. "Just" follow the manufacturers instructions and "pip" it into place (no, not the old DEC PIP peripheral to peripheral connector, the new python software package installer). Failure to launch:

 

$ pip install qwiic_bme280
error: externally-managed-environment

 

Oh, darn, this didn't work, and spit out a bunch of scary messages, especially this last dooms-day-scenario:

 

You can override this, at the risk of breaking \
 your Python installation or OS,  \
 by passing --break-system-packages. \
hint: See PEP 668 for the detailed specification.

 

"Break system packages" sounds ominous. Maybe don't take that approach. 

pip breakage

(screenshot from terminal session)

A more staid detective would go read the reference manuals, consult others online, re-read the source code, then diligently make necessary repairs. Others more reactive might literally check with their vendors and ask what's up with this. Investigators with a hacking tendency would start typing likely fixes and rout/route around the damage. Command line use of a venv turned out to be fairly simple:

 

python3 -m venv venv
venv/bin/pip3 install sparkfun-qwiic-bme280

[...]
Successfully installed \
 smbus2-0.5.0 \
 sparkfun-qwiic-bme280-0.9.0 \
 sparkfun-qwiic-i2c-1.0.0

 

No doubt more experienced Python developers are aghast at the hammer approach I used.

Getting readings at the command line would now work with 2 sensors; the new one shows:

 

$ venv/bin/python3.11  /path/to/sparkfun.py  temperature_celsius
16.2118

 

Fine, a 3-pipe problem rather than a trivial 2-pipe one. Except the whole intent is to automate sensor data collection, not have to run a command line manually. I'll leave out the messy trial-and-error details, other than to share that the Zabbix process debug steps boil down to seeing whether a parameter shows as "not supported" or returns an error message such as "got string, expecting a number" in the console, and checking the agent logs for clues (like: "The Qwiic BME280 device isn't connected to the system. Please check your connection" when I used the wrong sensor).

Stuffed inside an old shell script are new links to the monitor logic.

 

/path/venv/python3 /path/sparkfun-venv.py temperature_celsius

 

The middle section didn't need to have any virtual environment goo but for the sake of my future self looking at these sensors after the next incompatibility will possibly recall why.

Before going to the trouble of writing calculation or trigger steps in parameter definitions I used a handy UI feature in Zabbix that overlays 2 measurements in one chart. First, make sure both metrics are stable:

2 charts

(screenshot from Zabbix web client)

Then, add the second parameter to a dashboard widget. I didn't muck around with colors, lines, or transparencies, taking the defaults as good enough for the quick quality control / QC check.

2 temperature sensors on 1 chart

(screenshot from Zabbix web client)

Next problems to solve?

  • The 2 gauges differ under "no flow" states by over 1 degree.
  • Alerts/triggers
  • Validation/calibration

Calibration

Back in the last century, my polytechnic/polymath education included calibrating thermometers by determining how close their readings were to freezing boiling water temperatures. A memorable lesson in theory versus practice, tossing aside the novice assumption that a device always shows accurate values. How many degrees or tenths-of-degrees plus or minus the correct reading? (let's skip over side issues such as does water boil at exactly 100 degrees Celsius at any specific elevation or location).

What I'd probably investigate next is recording the temperature at the taps compared to the utility room infrastructure, particularly after hearing anecdotal comments such as "the kitchen sink faucet is not hot enough."

NOTES / REFERENCES

Shell:

  • https://znil.net/index.php/Zabbix:Template_Raspberry_Pi
  • https://znil.net/index.php?title=Temperatur_/_Spannung_etc._des_Raspberry_Pi_selbst_auslesen

Python:

  • http://rptl.io/venv
  • https://docs.python.org/3/library/venv.html
  • https://peps.python.org/pep-0668/
  • https://github.com/sparkfun/qwiic_bme280_py

SAP Community posts:

  • The ABAP Detective Takes The Summer Heat
  • The ABAP Detective Gets Their Clock Cleaned
  • The ABAP Detective Takes The Heat, or How To Cleanse Data

Also:

  • Intra home detectors

entry.title: No confirmed schedule lines even though there is available stock
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have enough stock for a material, but I get no confirmed schedule lines.

I have searched and read basically any post about this, but nothing worked.

Any tips?

VA05:

MMBE:

CO09:

entry.title: No Work Orders Downloaded
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to implement SSAM 2410 and have reviewed lots of documentation but still unable to download work orders and notifications to mobile app (Functional Locations and Equipment have downloaded successfully, 'out of box')

I have set the following for work orders

Perona - Maintenance Tech (Pro)

OMDO  Filter - SAM2410_Order_Type with category, order type and planning plant

OMDO  Filter - SAM2410_Work_Order_Generic with main wc, ord type, planning Plant PM_Phase, WO assignment Type.

Have tried both Handler and Userparam rules

What am I missing?

Haven't touched Notification ODMO settings yet but out of box is also not working.

Thanks in advance for any suggestions.

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Manage Situation object, Manage Situation - ex...
entry.content:
@ana_goncalvesI do added role SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC and S4SE_CUST_EXPERT_CONFIGURATION in my role.

but still cannot find Manage Object Extended. pls advise more.

entry.title: WBS Authorizations UE
entry.content:
I want to use UE for WBS Authorizations - I've implemented : FUNCTION EXIT_SAPLCNAU_002.

When I change WBS it doesn't go through this function.

When I debug it I see that it happens because of this line:

Meaning flg_exit_active-cnau_002 is empty.

How may I make it non empty please?

Thanks

Noy

entry.title: Re: Unable to Rename Apps in SAP Build Apps - Free Edition
entry.content:
In the free edition, after logging in, I am redirected to this page:

In the free edition, do I have access to the page in your screenshot? If so, how can I access it?

entry.title: Standard Content in SEN for SAP RAR module
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there standard content available for RAR module in SAP Enable Now?

Best,

Ajith K J

entry.title: One Network, Many Journeys
entry.content:
SAP Business Network began as a procurement platform – still its leading force today - then slowly started touching on additional operations. Today, the network includes supply chain, logistic and asset management transactions. As a result, the types of trading partners playing a role on the network flourished beyond the typical supplier-buyer relationship. Millions of trading partners interact daily in this constantly evolving environment, and not everyone had the same journey of joining SAP Business Network.

For organizations in need of a more holistic view of the network, we have put together a summarizing presentation exploring diverse journeys and multiple potential access points that suppliers and trading partners take to join and use SAP Business Network.

Click on the image to open the document

This deck is designed to provide a broad overview and valuable insights of how trading partners collaborate on SAP Business Network, highlighting key touchpoints and engagements with our teams.


We hope you’ll find this document helpful to map out your past, present and future journeys on SAP Business Network.

entry.title: Re: Best Practices for SAP HANA DB Installation on SUSE L...
entry.content: no, SAPB1 on HANA requires SLD services to be installed on Linux OS.

entry.title: Re: Deficit of SL Unrestricted-use Message No. M7021
entry.content: Just a regular inbound delivery from a PO

entry.title: Story data dictionary (Not the old learning one)
entry.content:
Hi

I'm looking for the Story Data Dictionary please if anyone can help. So I can find fields as my search in Stories isn't working. I just want to be able to find fields without spending hours looking manually through tables.

I'm not referring to the Learning one as that's not for stories as the table names are very different. Although it would be good to have a learning story one as well for the future.

Thanks
